summary: No rate limiting, throttling, or request quotas implemented anywhere in the application.

tests.description: No rate limiting on public analysis endpoints
tests.expected: HTTP 429 after N requests per IP/minute
tests.actual: No SlowAPI, no custom middleware, no API gateway throttling detected
tests.evidence: main.py: only CORSMiddleware registered. requirements.txt: slowapi, limits, or rate-limit libraries not present.
tests.notes: An attacker can flood /analyze/screenshot or /analyze/voice endpoints to exhaust expensive OpenAI API credits (GPT-4o Vision + Whisper-1). Each request triggers a paid API call if Tesseract is unavailable. This is both a DoS vector and a financial abuse risk.

tests.description: No rate limiting on CSV export endpoint — can be called repeatedly to dump DB
tests.actual: No rate limiting, no authentication — unlimited calls allowed
tests.evidence: routes/admin.py line 69: no Depends() for rate limit or auth
tests.notes: Combined with zero auth, this allows continuous automated DB dumping.

tests.description: No IP-based blocking or brute-force protection
tests.actual: No IP tracking, no request counting, no block list
tests.evidence: main.py + all route files — no middleware for IP tracking
tests.notes: No protection against automated scanning or credential-stuffing if auth is added in the future.

tests.description: OpenAI API credit exhaustion via unlimited requests
tests.expected: Per-user or per-IP quota on AI-backed endpoints
tests.actual: Unlimited requests accepted; every screenshot/voice call may invoke gpt-4o or whisper-1
tests.evidence: services/screenshot_analyzer.py line 82-94: _ocr_openai() called when Tesseract unavailable. services/voice_analyzer.py likely calls Whisper API. No usage tracking.
tests.notes: A single script sending 1000 requests to /analyze/screenshot could cost hundreds of dollars in OpenAI API fees. This is a CRITICAL financial and operational risk.
